project_metadata.project_summary: Build a web-based Revenue Assurance Dashboard for a telecom finance team that ingests daily CDR and billing feeds, reconciles usage vs billed amounts, and highlights revenue leakage. Users (analysts, managers, auditors) access KPIs and drill-down reports by region, product, and time. Solution must support role-based access, performant queries, and daily batch processing.

tasks.title: Design schema and implement CDR/billing ingestion pipeline
tasks.description: Design PostgreSQL schema for CDRs, billing records, and reconciled results. Implement FastAPI endpoints and Celery jobs to ingest daily CDR and billing feeds (CSV/Parquet), validate, and persist data with basic error logging.
acceptance_criteria: PostgreSQL schema created with tables for CDRs, billing, and reconciled records using Alembic migrations
API or scheduled job ingests sample CDR and billing files and stores at least 100k records without errors
Invalid records are logged with reason and skipped without stopping the batch
Ingestion can be triggered via a single CLI or API call and is idempotent per batch date
deliverables: Database schema and Alembic migration scripts
FastAPI ingestion endpoints or CLI commands
Celery worker configuration and tasks for batch ingestion
Technical README describing ingestion process and file formats

tasks.title: Implement reconciliation rules engine and role-based access control
tasks.description: Build reconciliation logic comparing network usage vs billed amounts with configurable thresholds. Implement JWT-based auth, user management, and role-based access (analyst, manager, auditor) for backend APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Reconciliation job computes mismatches by subscriber, product, region, and stores leakage records
Thresholds for acceptable variance are configurable per product via DB or config table
JWT-based authentication implemented with password hashing and token expiry
RBAC enforces that only managers see aggregate KPIs and auditors have read-only access
deliverables: Reconciliation service module with unit tests for at least 3 rule types
FastAPI endpoints to trigger reconciliation and fetch leakage records
User, role, and permission models with migration scripts
Auth and RBAC middleware integrated into FastAPI

tasks.title: Develop KPI dashboard and caching of reconciliation results
tasks.description: Implement React dashboard showing revenue at risk, recovery rate, and open leakage cases. Integrate with backend APIs and use Redis caching for aggregated KPI queries to ensure fast load times.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard page loads KPIs in under 2 seconds for dataset of 1M records using cached aggregates
KPIs correctly reflect backend calculations for revenue at risk, recovery rate, and open cases
KPI widgets auto-refresh when new reconciliation batch completes
Unauthorized users cannot access KPI endpoints or dashboard route
deliverables: React components for KPI cards and summary charts
FastAPI endpoints for aggregated KPI data with Redis caching
Integration tests verifying KPI values against sample datasets
Documentation on cache invalidation strategy tied to batch completion

tasks.title: Implement drill-down reports by region, product, and time
tasks.description: Create interactive tables and charts allowing users to drill from KPIs into leakage details by region, product, and time period, with filters, pagination, and export to CSV.
acceptance_criteria: Users can filter leakage records by region, product, date range, and status with server-side pagination
Drill-down navigation from KPI widgets to filtered detail views works without page reloads
CSV export produces files matching visible filters and columns
Auditor role has read-only access to all drill-down views
deliverables: React pages for leakage list and detail views with filters
Backend endpoints supporting filtered, paginated queries
CSV export endpoint and UI trigger
UX documentation describing drill-down flows and role visibility

tasks.title: End-to-end QA, performance testing, and risk assessment
tasks.description: Define test scenarios, execute end-to-end and performance tests, validate security and data integrity, and document operational risks and mitigation for the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end test suite covers ingestion, reconciliation, KPIs, and drill-down for at least 10 core scenarios
Load test demonstrates system can process daily batch of 1M CDRs within agreed time window
Security review confirms RBAC, JWT, and data access controls behave as designed
Risk register created with at least 8 identified risks and mitigation actions
deliverables: Automated test scripts (API and UI) and test report
Performance test results with bottleneck analysis and tuning recommendations
Security and access control validation report
Risk register and go-live readiness checklist

raw_plan.reasoning: The plan focuses first on reliable ingestion and reconciliation, then on KPI visualization and drill-down, aligning with the telecom finance use case and chosen stack while keeping scope achievable in two phases.
